thread.title_full: BJP Politician Sparks Controversy by Refusing to Follow Seat Belt Laws | Ludhiana News - Times of India

author: Times Of India
title: BJP Politician Sparks Controversy by Refusing to Follow Seat Belt Laws | Ludhiana News - Times of India
text: Ludhiana: Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) member Jatinder Goryan has been charged with misbehaving with traffic police on Pakhowal Road when pulled over for not wearing seat belt. None of the five people in his car had worn it. A video of the heated argument went viral. City Traffic Zone officer in-charge Kuldeep Singh has claimed that Goryan when signalled to stop, the politician parked his car in the middle of the road, causing a traffic disruption. Goryan then confronted the cops in an alleged misbehaving tone. As a result of the incident, the Sadar police station registered a case against Goryan and his companions under charges of obstructing a public servant and causing public nuisance, following a complaint from ASI Kuldeep Singh.We also published the following articles recentlyLouisville's Tayon Holloway has been suspended indefinitely following his arrest for charges of strangulation and domestic violence assaultUniversity of Louisville sophomore defensive back, Tayon Holloway, faces suspension following his arrest for allegedly strangling a woman. Police reports indicate that Holloway physically assaulted the woman after she attempted to stop him from smoking marijuana. The incident occurred after Louisville's loss to Stanford, where Holloway's performance was marked by a crucial penalty.1154354461 held for damaging police kiosk, obstructing cops amid public disputeA man was arrested in Kolkata for vandalizing a traffic police kiosk and obstructing officers. The incident occurred after police detained a man accused of misbehaving with a woman on a crowded bus. This marks the third attack on police in recent days, following assaults on officers at Regent Park and on the Garden Reach flyover.115456099Patna traffic police to cancel licences of habitual road rule violatorsIn a crackdown on repeat traffic offenders, Patna traffic police are taking stringent measures. Over 133 drivers face license cancellation for accumulating over 20 fines within six months. An additional 5,691 individuals could have their licenses suspended for multiple violations, including speeding and wrong-side driving.115366530
